title: Knee Injuries and Disorders
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: Knee pain can come from overuse, minor injuries, arthritis, inflammation, or other disorders.
Home care may include rest, activity modification, ice or elevation when appropriate, and clinician-directed medicines.
Warning signs include inability to walk, severe swelling, redness, fever, deformity, neurologic or circulation changes, and worsening pain.
Clinician assessment determines when imaging, aspiration, orthopedic care, or a different pathway is needed.
